title: Garlicky Polish Chicken Wings Pasta
description: A crowd-pleasing Polish dish featuring juicy chicken wings, a glassy-jelly pasta-water-emulsion, and dairy-free-friendly sauce. This recipe serves a crowd and is easy to make ahead of time.

ingredients.name: hot sauce
ingredients.notes: such as sriracha or hot sauce of choice

ingredients.name: dairy-free mayonnaise
ingredients.notes: such as vegenaise or follow your heart

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together flour, hot sauce, and apple cider vinegar to make the marinade.
Add the chicken wings to the marinade and toss to coat. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes or up to 2 hours.
Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil and spray with cooking spray. Remove the chicken wings from the marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off.
Bake the chicken wings in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes or until cooked through and slightly browned.
While the chicken wings are baking, cook the pasta according to package instructions until al dente.
In a large skillet, heat 1/4 cup of vegetable oil over medium-high heat. Add the onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the cooked pasta to the skillet, tossing to combine with the onion and garlic. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Once the chicken wings are done, toss them in a separate skillet with the remaining 1/4 cup of vegetable oil to coat.
To assemble the dish, place a portion of pasta on a plate, followed by 2-3 chicken wings, and top with a dollop of dairy-free mayonnaise.
Sprinkle chopped parsley over the top for garnish and serve immediately.
To make ahead, cook the pasta and chicken wings separately and store in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours. Assemble the dish just before serving.
Reheat the chicken wings in the oven or in a skillet on the stovetop if needed.
Reheat the pasta in the microwave or on the stovetop if needed.
